PreToolUse.description: Block Write/Edit tools during plan/verify/compound stages — only allow in implement stage

PreToolUse.matcher: tool == "Bash" && tool_input.command matches "(npm run dev|pnpm( run)? dev|yarn dev|bun run dev)"

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""')

# Block dev servers that aren't run in tmux
echo '[Hook] BLOCKED: Dev server must run in tmux for log access' >&2
echo '[Hook] Use this command instead:' >&2
echo "[Hook] tmux new-session -d -s dev 'npm run dev'" >&2
echo '[Hook] Then: tmux attach -t dev' >&2
exit 1

PreToolUse.description: Block dev servers outside tmux - ensures you can access logs

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
if [ -z "$TMUX" ]; then
  echo '[Hook] Consider running in tmux for session persistence' >&2
  echo '[Hook] tmux new -s dev  |  tmux attach -t dev' >&2
fi
echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Reminder to use tmux for long-running commands
Pause before git push to review changes

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [[ "$file_path" =~ \.(md|txt)$ ]] && [[ ! "$file_path" =~ (README|CLAUDE|AGENT|SKILL)\.md$ ]]; then
  echo "[Hook] BLOCKED: Unnecessary documentation file creation" >&2
  echo "[Hook] File: $file_path" >&2
  echo "[Hook] Use README.md or AGENT.md for documentation instead" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Block creation of random .md files - keeps docs consolidated

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
COUNTER_FILE="/tmp/claude-tool-count-$PPID"
THRESHOLD=${COMPACT_THRESHOLD:-50}
if [ -f "$COUNTER_FILE" ]; then COUNT=$(cat "$COUNTER_FILE"); COUNT=$((COUNT + 1)); else COUNT=1; fi
echo "$COUNT" > "$COUNTER_FILE"
if [ "$COUNT" -eq "$THRESHOLD" ]; then echo '[StrategicCompact] Reached '$THRESHOLD' tool calls' >&2; echo '[StrategicCompact] Consider /compact if at a logical breakpoint' >&2; fi
if [ "$COUNT" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ] && [ $(((COUNT - THRESHOLD) % 25)) -eq 0 ]; then echo "[StrategicCompact] $COUNT tool calls - /compact reminder" >&2; fi
cat

PreToolUse.description: Suggest manual compaction at logical intervals
Validate file content hash before Edit — advisory staleness warning
Schema-based tool input validation — Phase 1 advisory only
RTK auto-intercept — transparently rewrites CLI commands through RTK proxy when available (R015 advisory)
Block rule file deletion — requires individual user confirmation per rule (R001 safety)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
agent_type=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.subagent_type // "unknown"')
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.model // "inherit"')
desc=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.description // ""' | head -c 40)
resume=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.resume // empty')
if [ -n "$resume" ]; then
  echo "─── [Resume] ${agent_type}:${model} | ${desc} ───" >&2
else
  echo "─── [Spawn] ${agent_type}:${model} | ${desc} ───" >&2
fi
echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: HUD statusline + R010 git delegation guard + R018 Agent Teams advisor on Agent/Task spawn

SessionStart.description: Interactive oh-my-customcode update check at session start (#752)
Check codex CLI and Agent Teams availability at session start
Scan TODO.md files for stale items at session start
Lightweight project profile staleness check for adaptive harness (#831)
Auto-detect and fix previous session issues at start (#838)

UserPromptSubmit.description: Advisory pre-processing of user input — skill matching hints and intent analysis
Inject session auto-fix findings into first user prompt (#838)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
agent_type=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.agent_type // "unknown"')
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model // "inherit"')
desc=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.description // ""' | head -c 40)
echo "─── [SubagentStart] ${agent_type}:${model} | ${desc} ───" >&2
echo "$input"

SubagentStart.description: HUD display + agent start time recording for stall detection (R009)

hooks.prompt: A background subagent just completed. Check if there are pending workflow steps that depend on this result. If the previous subagent FAILED, do NOT auto-continue — report the failure and wait for user input. If the previous step succeeded and there are pending steps, proceed automatically. If no pending steps, report results and wait. Safety: The file /tmp/.claude-loop-count-$PPID tracks auto-continue count. After 3 consecutive auto-continues without user interaction, pause and ask the user before proceeding.

SubagentStop.description: Record agent outcomes + auto-continue workflow on subagent completion

CwdChanged.description: Auto-detect project type on working directory change (v2.1.83+)

FileChanged.description: Validate externally modified files — advisory staleness warning (v2.1.83+)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
msg=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.message // ""' 2>/dev/null)
if [ -n "$msg" ]; then
  echo "[Hook] Notification: $msg" >&2
fi
echo "$input"

Notification.description: Display notifications for long-running operation completions

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
teammate=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.teammate_id // "unknown"' 2>/dev/null)
echo "[Hook] TeammateIdle: $teammate — available for reassignment" >&2
echo "$input"

TeammateIdle.description: Track idle Agent Teams members for work reassignment

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
task_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.task_id // ""' 2>/dev/null)
desc=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.description // ""' 2>/dev/null | head -c 60)
echo "[Hook] TaskCreated: $task_id — $desc" >&2
echo "$input"

TaskCreated.description: Log task creation events for Agent Teams coordination

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
task_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.task_id // ""' 2>/dev/null)
result=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.result // ""' 2>/dev/null | head -c 60)
echo "[Hook] TaskCompleted: $task_id — $result" >&2
echo "$input"

TaskCompleted.description: Log task completion events for Agent Teams coordination

hooks.prompt:
Context compacted. RULES ACTIVE.

R007: next response MUST start with ┌─ Agent: claude (default) / └─ Task: {task}
R008: before EVERY tool call → [claude][model] → Tool: Name / Target: path
R010: write→delegate to subagent; orchestrator read-only (Read/Glob/Grep); git→mgr-gitnerd
R009: 2+ independent tasks → parallel agents, same message
R018: 3+ agents OR review cycle → Agent Teams
R000: user communication in Korean (한국어); code/files in English — NON-NEGOTIABLE

Autonomous mode: if /tmp/.claude-autonomous-$PPID exists → simple git (add/commit/push) may run directly; Write/Edit still delegates.
bypassPermissions does NOT persist across compaction — re-enable if tasks seem blocked.

Re-read CLAUDE.md NOW.

PostCompact.description: Reinforce enforced rules after context compaction — prevents rule amnesia (v2.1.76+)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.command')

if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'gh pr create'; then
  output=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_output.output // ""')
  pr_url=$(echo "$output" | grep -oE 'https://github.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/[0-9]+')
  
  if [ -n "$pr_url" ]; then
    echo "[Hook] PR created: $pr_url" >&2
    echo "[Hook] Checking GitHub Actions status..." >&2
    repo=$(echo "$pr_url" | sed -E 's|https://github.com/([^/]+/[^/]+)/pull/[0-9]+|\1|')
    pr_num=$(echo "$pr_url" | sed -E 's|.*/pull/([0-9]+)|\1|')
    echo "[Hook] To review PR: gh pr review $pr_num --repo $repo" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Log PR URL and provide review command after PR creation

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  if command -v prettier >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    prettier --write "$file_path" 2>&1 | head -5 >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Auto-format JS/TS files with Prettier after edits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
  project_root="$dir"
  while [ "$project_root" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$project_root/package.json" ]; do
    project_root=$(dirname "$project_root")
  done
  
  if [ -f "$project_root/tsconfig.json" ]; then
    cd "$project_root" && npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false 2>&1 | grep "$file_path" | head -10 >&2 || true
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  console_logs=$(grep -n "console\\.log" "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || true)
  
  if [ -n "$console_logs" ]; then
    echo "[Hook] WARNING: console.log found in $file_path" >&2
    echo "$console_logs" | head -5 >&2
    echo "[Hook] Remove console.log before committing" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Warn about console.log statements after edits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  if command -v gofmt >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    gofmt -w "$file_path" 2>&1 >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Auto-format Go files with gofmt after edits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  # Format with ruff
  if command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    ruff format --quiet "$file_path" 2>&1 >&2
    ruff check --fix --quiet "$file_path" 2>&1 >&2 || true
    echo "[Hook] Python formatted with ruff" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Auto-format and lint Python files with ruff after edits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  # Type check with ty
  if command -v ty >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
    project_root="$dir"
    while [ "$project_root" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$project_root/pyproject.toml" ]; do
      project_root=$(dirname "$project_root")
    done
    
    if [ -f "$project_root/pyproject.toml" ]; then
      cd "$project_root" && ty check "$file_path" 2>&1 | head -10 >&2 || true
    else
      ty check "$file_path" 2>&1 | head -10 >&2 || true
    fi
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Type check Python files with ty after edits

PostToolUse.matcher: tool == "Edit" || tool == "Write" || tool == "Bash" || tool == "Task" || tool == "Agent" || tool == "Read" || tool == "Glob" || tool == "Grep"
PostToolUse.description: Context budget advisor — track tool usage patterns and advise ecomode activation
Detect repetitive failure loops and advise recovery strategies
Advisory cost cap monitoring — warn when session cost approaches configurable limit
Store content hashes for Read operations — enables Edit staleness detection
Detect potential secrets in Bash/Read/Grep output — advisory warning only
Append-only audit log for state-changing tool operations

Stop.description: Final console.log audit and session diagnostics before session ends
Batch-save agent outcomes to eval-core DB on session end (advisory, exit 0)
Auto-extract failure patterns from session outcomes (advisory, exit 0)
Detect reusable skill candidates from session task outcomes (advisory, exit 0)

hooks.prompt: Session-end memory checkpoint (R011 enforcement). Check if the session-end memory save workflow was completed: 1) sys-memory-keeper was delegated to update MEMORY.md 2) claude-mem save was attempted via ToolSearch + mcp__plugin_claude-mem_mcp-search__save_memory. Note: episodic-memory auto-indexes after session — no manual action needed. Decision rules (evaluate in order): 1. If session had no explicit session-end signal (quick question, no memory work): APPROVE immediately. 2. If BOTH steps were already attempted (success or failure both count): APPROVE. 3. If MCP tools unavailable after ToolSearch attempt: APPROVE with note. 4. If steps were NOT yet performed: PERFORM THEM NOW (maximum 1 attempt each): a. Delegate to sys-memory-keeper to update MEMORY.md — call Agent tool with sys-memory-keeper. b. Attempt claude-mem save — ToolSearch for save_memory tool, then call it. c. After completion (success or failure), APPROVE stop. IMPORTANT: Do NOT block indefinitely. After 1 attempt per step, always APPROVE. IMPORTANT: Do NOT loop — if you already performed these steps in this same stop hook evaluation, APPROVE.

Stop.description: Enforce R011 session-end memory saves — auto-perform if not yet done (episodic-memory auto-indexes)
